name: Hologram
description: A 3D architecture viewer. Push one abstraction level at a time as a flat nodes-and-edges scene (its 'architecture' context document) and the pane renders it as a navigable subway-style map; drill into nodes for child scenes. Read back the user's selection and drive the camera via the 'command' document. Use describe_plugin for the full authoring schema.

contextExports.label: Hologram architecture
contextExports.description: CONCEPT — a hologram is a readable MAP of ONE abstraction level, not a wiring schematic. Pick the ~5-12 components that tell the story at this altitude; let `kind` carry each node's role (it chooses the shape and color, so the picture reads at a glance); let `edges` be the data-flow narrative; give depth by making nodes drillable into child scenes rather than cramming everything onto one level. If it wouldn't read as a clean subway map, it's two levels — split it. Name and summarize for a human looking at the picture. TECH — this is the authoring door for the 3D viewer: send ONE COMPLETE abstraction level as a flat scene. Schema: {"format":"archviz/2","id":"slug","title":"...","subtitle":"..."?,"nodes":[...],"edges":[...],"layout":{...}?}. NODE = {"id","label","kind","summary"?,"expandable"?,"childrenPath"?,"details"?}. Set `kind` and let it derive the glyph, color, size, AND position — do NOT hand-author pos/glyph/color/size. kinds: input, embedding, position, attention, norm, ffn, mlp, output, recurrent, vector, matrix, activation, sum, expert, router, moe, conv, cache, data, control, memory, tool, retrieval, db, index (unknown kind => neutral slab). Labels are plain strings (no literal \n). EDGE = {"a":"srcId","b":"dstId","residual":true?} — directed data-flow a->b; keys are a/b, NOT from/to. Edges drive auto-layout ranking and are inspectable. LAYOUT (optional) = {"type":"flow"|"layered"|"stack"|"grid"|"radial", ...}; default is a vertical `flow` chain, `layered` ranks by edges and fans across 3D. Keep each scene SMALL and FLAT (~5-12 nodes). HIERARCHY IS SEPARATE SCENES, never nested: mark a node `expandable:true` to make it drillable; on drill the pane loads that node's child scene, and if none exists it asks you via the Hologram selection channel's request:{action:'expand',path:[...]} to push the child level through THIS channel. Do NOT embed child nodes inside a parent. The Hologram selection read-back is a LOSSY flat index (id/label/kind/expandable only, no edges/details) — author from THIS schema; never reverse-engineer it from that echo. details? (rich inspector blocks) = [{"type":"markdown","title","md"} | {"type":"code","title","language","source"} | {"type":"keyValue","title","items":[{"k","v"}]} | {"type":"list","title","ordered":true?,"items":[...]}]. See plugins/hologram/scenes/*.json for worked examples.

contextExports.label: Hologram command
contextExports.description: Shape: {"action":"focus"|"select","target":["sceneId","childId","nodeId"]}. target is a full path — last segment is the node, earlier segments are the cached scene path (omit for the current level, e.g. target:["mha1"]). 'focus' highlights and flies the camera in; 'select' only highlights. Read the loaded scene's valid targets from the 'index' field of the Hologram selection channel. A path whose level isn't loaded retries until you push it via the architecture channel. Legacy {"nodeId","path"} still works.
